channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Ex-keeper Manninger dies after car hit by train
item.description: Former Arsenal goalkeeper Alex Manninger dies at the age of 48 after his car is struck by a train.

item.title: Barca say refereeing 'errors' cost them Champions League semi-final spot
item.description: Barcelona say refereeing "errors" cost them a place in the semi-finals of the Champions League, and have caused "significant sporting and financial harm to the club".

item.title: Italy made me a manager when England 'discouraged' me - Cole
item.description: Ashley Cole won 107 caps for England, seventh on the all‑time list, but says he was "discouraged" by those in the football pyramid from becoming a head coach.

item.title: Why Silva will be remembered as a Man City legend
item.description: BBC Sport takes a look at Bernardo Silva's glittering career at Manchester City and why fans will never forget him.

item.title: Chelsea midfielder Caicedo agrees new contract
item.description: Chelsea midfielder Moises Caicedo agrees a lucrative new contract at Stamford Bridge as the club seeks to reward its high performers.

item.title: Sutton's predictions v boxer Molly McCann & the Boo Radleys
item.description: Chris Sutton takes on boxer Molly McCann and The Boo Radley's frontman Sice Rowbottom plus the BBC readers and AI with his predictions for this weekend's Premier League fixtures.

item.title: Bellingham says Camavinga red 'a joke' as Real fury grows
item.description: Real Madrid's Jude Bellingham says the decision to send off team-mate Eduardo Camavinga during their 4-3 Champions League loss to Bayern Munich was "a joke".

item.title: Fifa blamed for $100 World Cup trains from New York
item.description: New Jersey Governor Mikie Sherrill accuses Fifa of failing to provide funding, which means fans must foot the bill for high transport costs at the World Cup this summer.

item.title: How Wubben-Moy has stepped into Williamson's shoes
item.description: England defender Lotte Wubben-Moy took her chance when she was handed a rare start in Tuesday's victory over Spain in Leah Williamson's absence.

item.title: Messi buys fifth-tier Spanish club Cornella
item.description: Argentina World Cup winner Lionel Messi becomes the new owner of Catalan club Cornella.

item.title: A big topic for others, not for her - Eta on groundbreaking head coach role
item.description: New Union Berlin head coach Marie-Louise Eta discusses becoming the first female to manage a men's team in one of Europe's top five leagues.

item.title: 'I leave as one more of you' - but how do Man City replace departing Silva?
item.description: Manchester City will lose the services of their dependable captain next season as Bernardo Silva announced he will leave the club when his contract expires this summer.

item.title: Seven goals, fastest strike and two red cards - Bayern-Real delivers drama
item.description: The quickest strike of this season's Champions League, seven goals, two red cards and the 15-time winners exiting in anger - once again the knockout stages of Europe's elite tournament delivered drama in spades.

item.title: Ekitike to miss rest of season and World Cup with ruptured Achilles
item.description: France striker Hugo Ekitike will miss the World Cup after suffering a rupture of the Achilles tendon during Liverpool's Champions League defeat by Paris St-Germain on Tuesday.

item.title: Play-off pending? How a draw at the Etihad could set up thrilling finale
item.description: Man City's meeting against Arsenal may go a long way to deciding the destination of the Premier League trophy in May, but how close a finish could it be?

item.title: The best free hit team for FPL's double gameweek
item.description: The season's biggest double gameweek is here and it is a chance to triple up on Manchester City and Bournemouth assets, writes Thomas Woods.

item.title: Captain Silva to leave Man City at end of season
item.description: Manchester City captain Bernardo Silva will leave the club when his contract expires at the end of this season.

item.title: Bayern strike late to beat Real Madrid in seven-goal thriller
item.description: Bayern Munich leave it late in a back-and-forth classic to beat Real Madrid 4-3 to set up a meeting with Paris St-Germain in the semi-finals of the Champions League.

item.title: Why both Champions League semi-finals are hard to call
item.description: Champions League analysts Stephen Warnock, Nedum Onouha and Julien Laurens look at why the semi-finals between PSG & Bayern Munich and Atletico Madrid & Arsenal are both really hard to call who will win.

item.title: 'He's done it!' - Diaz scores stunner to earn Bayern late win
item.description: Luis Diaz scores a powerful strike from the edge of the box to break 10-man Real Madrid's resistance, as Bayern Munich take a late lead in their Champions League quarter-final tie.

item.title: Arsenal through to semi-finals with cagey draw against Sporting
item.description: Arsenal play out an unconvincing goalless draw at home with Sporting to qualify for the semi-finals of the Champions League, where they will meet Atletico Madrid.

item.title: 'Goodness me' - Neuer mistake gifts Guler opener after 35 seconds
item.description: Arda Guler pounces on a loose pass from Manuel Neuer to level the tie between Real Madrid and Bayern Munich in the Champions League quarter-final second leg at the Allianz Arena.

item.title: £124m spent but Newcastle no closer to striker solution
item.description: Newcastle United spent £124m to replace departing Alexander Isak, but Eddie Howe's men now seem no closer to finding their striking solution.

item.title: Liverpool lead Senesi race - Thursday's gossip
item.description: Bournemouth's Marcos Senesi on Liverpool's radar, Reds also interested in Newcastle forward Anthony Gordon, while Manchester City are tracking Feyenoord defender Givairo Read.

item.title: Time running out to make Champions League - Rosenior
item.description: Chelsea manager Liam Rosenior says his side are running out of time to qualify for next season's Champions League campaign.

item.title: Brentford to host World Sevens as Villa, Man Utd, Spurs & West Ham join
item.description: Aston Villa and Manchester United will compete in this year's Women's World Sevens tournament, which will be hosted in Brentford.

item.title: Arsenal limp to semis but Arteta wanted fire - does style matter?
item.description: Mikel Arteta wanted "fire" from his Arsenal players but instead got a sluggish performance as the Gunners did what was needed to reach the Champions League semi-finals.

item.title: How qualifying for Champions League could affect Man Utd's loanees
item.description: Man Utd want to qualify for the Champions League but if they do it will make Andre Onana and Marcus Rashford harder to sell.

item.title: Northern Ireland to face World Cup-bound France
item.description: Northern Ireland will face two-time world champions France in a friendly in Lille in the lead-up to the latter's World Cup campaign.

item.title: Wood must manage knee issue for the rest of his life
item.description: Nottingham Forest striker Chris Wood will have to manage his knee injury for the rest of his life.

item.title: Dembele double sends Liverpool crashing out of Champions League
item.description: Ousmane Dembele's second-half double puts an end to a spirited Liverpool performance, as Paris Saint-Germain seal their place in the Champions League semi-finals with a 2-0 second-leg win at Anfield.

item.title: Is Slot still searching for his ideal Liverpool starting XI?
item.description: Champions League analysts Stephen Warnock and Nedum Onuoha feel Arne Slot's Liverpool do not have a definitive starting XI and style of play like other clubs around Europe, following their elimination from the competition at the hands of PSG.

item.title: Atleti hold off Barca comeback to reach semi-finals
item.description: Atletico Madrid survive a 2-1 second leg defeat to reach the semi-finals of the Champions League, as ten-man Barcelona are eliminated 3-2 on aggregate at the Wanda Metropolitano.

item.title: How Bayern Munich's wingers took the game to Real Madrid
item.description: Champions League analysts Stephen Warnock, Nedum Onuoha and Julien Laurens look at how Michael Olise and Luis Diaz caused Real Madrid's defence constant problems after they helped Bayern Munich reach the semi-finals.

item.title: How PSG's 'engine room' were the key to Liverpool win
item.description: BBC Sport pundit Stephen Warnock believes PSG's Joao Neves, Vitinha and Warren Zaire-Emery are "the best three midfielders" in the world after their influential role in the French champions' 2-0 win over Liverpool at Anfield in the Champions League quarter-finals.

item.title: Real Madrid set for rare trophyless season - will Arbeloa pay the price?
item.description: After losing out to Bayern Munich in a thrilling Champions League quarter-final, Real Madrid face the prospect of a trophyless season that could cost Alvaro Arbeloa his job.

item.title: How to follow the Premier League on the BBC this weekend
item.description: BBC Sport Ask Me Anything explains how to follow the Premier League on the BBC this week.

item.title: The 24-year-old manager who has won a top-flight promotion
item.description: Having worked with the Real Madrid Foundation as a teenager, Jordan Hadaway has now led financially-challenged Llandudno to Wales' top-flight.

item.title: Sheff Wed to start next season with 15-point penalty, bidder told
item.description: The EFL has informed the preferred bidder for Sheffield Wednesday that the club will start next season in League One with a 15-point penalty.

item.title: Ex-Arsenal director on growth under 'red arrow' Arteta
item.description: Former Arsenal technical director James Ellis gives insight into life at Arsenal working with Mikel Arteta.

item.title: Is Arteta right in talk around Arsenal form?
item.description: Champions League analysts Stephen Warnock, Nedum Onouha and Julien Laurens believe Mikel Arteta is trying to "convince" the Arsenal fanbase that concerns around his team's form are overblown, after Arsenal qualified for the semi-finals in nervy fashion against Sporting.

item.title: Wood returns for Nottingham Forest after knee injury
item.description: Nottingham Forest striker Chris Wood says it felt like there was "no light at the end of the tunnel" during parts of his recovery.

item.title: Scotland score in stoppage time to salvage draw with Belgium
item.description: Watch the highlights as Scotland score in stoppage time to salvage a World Cup qualification draw with Belgium.

item.title: 'It's make or break for him' - is it silverware or the sack for Arteta?
item.description: Speaking on the Monday Night Club, former Premier League striker and BBC Sport pundit Chris Sutton says Arsenal head coach Mikel Arteta must win the Premier League or the Champions League this season or risk losing his job at the Emirates.

item.title: Okafor fires Leeds to first league win at Old Trafford since 1981
item.description: Leeds boost their Premier League survival hopes with a 2-1 win against 10-man Manchester United at Old Trafford.

item.title: Martinez red card 'shocking decision' - Carrick
item.description: Manchester United boss Michael Carrick says Lisandro Martinez's red card for pulling Dominic Calvert-Lewin's hair was a "shocking decision" during a 2-1 defeat by Leeds United.

item.title: Coaching or confidence - What should De Zerbi prioritise at Spurs?
item.description: Speaking on the Monday Night Club, former Premier League defender Nedum Onuoha says new Tottenham head coach Roberto de Zerbi is right to focus on raising morale rather than tactics as he looks to keep the club in the Premier League.

item.title: John Terry and Colchester - why now, and why are ex-pros buying clubs?
item.description: BBC Sport explores John Terry's imminent takeover of Colchester, why ex-pros are buying clubs, and what the feeling is among fans.

item.title: Bayern links and World Cup hopes - a big summer for Gordon
item.description: Anthony Gordon faces a big summer - with World Cup hopes and the possibility that Newcastle will need to sell him. So what comes next for the England forward?

item.title: What is an Achilles injury?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team explain what an Achilles injury is and why they are so serious.

item.title: Refereeing v Atletico 'a robbery' - Barca's Raphinha
item.description: Barcelona's exit from the Champions League quarter-finals was "a robbery", says their forward Raphinha.

item.title: Liverpool boss Slot 'surprised' Hillsborough Law not yet passed
item.description: The Liverpool manager said families "should not have to fight for the truth" of how loved ones died.

item.title: Former footballers to meet government over 'financial abuse'
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer has asked the Treasury to meet a group of retired footballers who say they were victims of "financial abuse".

item.title: Will Iraola's summer exit speed up managerial merry-go-round?
item.description: Andoni Iraola's decision to leave Bournemouth could make it a frantic summer in the Premier League's managerial market, says chief football writer Phil McNulty.

item.title: Three points off top, so why is Sutton ruling Celtic out?
item.description: BBC Scotland dissects the former Celtic striker's reasons and examines the substance behind them.

item.title: Ranking the biggest Premier League title slip-ups
item.description: With Arsenal desperate to banish their title-bottling demons, we look at the top 10 teams who have let Premier League leads slip.

item.title: A monumental night for Leeds - what does it mean in survival scrap?
item.description: A monumental victory over Manchester United at Old Trafford has given Leeds United a priceless lifeline in their quest for Premier League survival.

item.title: What is going on with Garnacho?
item.description: Alejandro Garnacho is struggling to stand out at Chelsea - so can the £40m signing from Manchester United still realise his potential at Stamford Bridge?

item.title: 54 years, 48 leagues, 2,000 grounds - the ultimate football odyssey
item.description: BBC Sport travels with the UK's ultimate groundhopper to the final match of a decades-long, history-making journey.

item.title: All done deals since February, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: How many points do you need to stay in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks into the number of points needed to avoid relegation from the Premier League

item.title: How are the Scottish Premiership post-split fixtures decided?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership split has again been a controversial talking point, so why does it exist and why is it so complicated?

item.title: Who is one card away from suspension in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who is currently nearing a suspension in the Premier League.

item.title: How does Scottish Premiership split work?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership title race has been a hot topic all season and it's about to become even more interesting with the league's split.

item.title: Have all home nations ever played in same World Cup?
item.description: With England and Scotland already qualified for the 2026 World Cup, Wales and Northern Ireland both face European qualifiers in March.

item.title: All you need to know about 2027 Women's World Cup
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team take a look at everything you need to know for the 2027 Women's World Cup.

item.title: When can Arsenal win the Premier League?
item.description: Arsenal are six points clear at the top of the Premier League, but when can they officially win the title?

item.title: Lennon annoyed at 'unfair' Dunfermline fixtures
item.description: Exasperated Neil Lennon describes his Dunfermline Athletic side's fixture schedule as "really unfair" leading up to Saturday's Scottish Cup semi-final against Falkirk.

item.title: St Mirren have proof they can beat Celtic - McLeish
item.description: St Mirren have been there, done it and are more than capable of doing it again in Sunday's Scottish Cup semi-final against Celtic, interim manager Craig McLeish says.

item.title: Scotland's last three-way title race - Dundee United's finest hour
item.description: Three sides are battling for the Scottish title this season, but when was the last time there such a close contest?

item.title: FC Basel postpone fixture after stadium fire
item.description: FC Basel's Swiss Super League match against FC Thun is postponed after a fire causes "significant damage" to Basel's St Jakob-Park stadium.

item.title: Spalletti signs new Juventus contract amid Italy links
item.description: Juventus manager Luciano Spalletti signs a two-year contract extension with the club, effectively ruling him out of a return to the Italy national team.

item.title: How Lens embraced underdog status to become PSG's title rivals
item.description: Lens have pushed PSG closer than anyone in Ligue 1 this season and, with the sides still to meet again, still hold an outside shot at the title.

item.title: Legendary manager Lucescu dies five days after resigning as Romania coach
item.description: Legendary manager Mircea Lucescu dies aged 80, five days after stepping down as Romania's head coach.

item.title: Success over Spain - but how can England get best out of classy James?
item.description: How can Sarina Wiegman get the best from one of England's most talented footballers, Lauren James?

item.title: Have Halliday's heroics started a new chapter for NI?
item.description: Northern Ireland aren't known for scoring a flurry of goals in games with just one in their past six matches prior to their World Cup qualifier against Malta.

item.title: Wales must take care of own business - Wilkinson
item.description: Rhian Wilkinson says Wales must "take care of our own business" before focusing on what looks set to be a key Women's World Cup qualifier against Czech Republic in June.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.

item.title: Napoli 'smell blood' & target Premier League's discarded stars
item.description: After Scott McTominay was a hit, Napoli went back to the Premier League for Kevin de Bruyne, could more players follow them to Serie A?
